commands.repair_reason: - [P2] There is one narrow, actionable blocker: make uncacheable auth state non-cacheable for persisted model catalog cache callers and add regression coverage.; Needs attention: A concrete auth-provider cache issue remains: uncacheable auth state can become a reusable persisted catalog cache key.; Review finding: [P1] Do not reuse catalog cache keys for uncacheable auth state — src/agents/models-config.ts:1857 (sha=2b3d939fd471110d70e5f9437a0d02bce43f874f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR unifies models-config content fingerprinting with provider-scoped targetProvider/targetModel short-circuiting, adds canonical SQLite auth-store reads, validates plugin catalog sidecars, wires embedded callers, and adds regression/proof scripts.

PR surface: Source +1550, Tests +2032, Other +744. Total +4326 across 23 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: the PR returns a stable uncacheable auth fingerprint and current callers use that fingerprint to read the persisted catalog cache before forcing models-config preparation. I did not run a mutating repro because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] There is one narrow, actionable blocker: make uncacheable auth state non-cacheable for persisted model catalog cache callers and add regression coverage.
